records.ety: [L. limbus border, edge in limbo on the border. Cf. Limb border.]

defs: An spiritual region where certain classes of souls were supposed to await the last judgment.
quotes: As far from help as Limbo is from bliss.
A Limbo large and broad, since called The Paradise of fools.
notes: ☞ The limbus patrum was considered as a place for the souls of good men who lived before the coming of our Savior. The limbus infantium was said to be a similar place for the souls of unbaptized infants. To these was added, in the popular belief, the limbus fatuorum, or fool's paradise, regarded as a receptacle of all vanity and nonsense.

defs: Hence: Any real or imaginary place of restraint or confinement; a prison; as, to put a man in limbo.

A state of waiting, or uncertainty, in which final judgment concerning the outcome of a decision is postponed, perhaps indefinitely; neglect for an indefinite time; as, the proposal was left in limbo while opponents and proponents refused to compromise.
A border or margin; as, the limbus of the cornea.
